Chemistry

Urinalysis

Test15-MAR-2020Reference RangeUnit
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip1.3 H0 - 0mg/dL
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip1.0 L1.005 - 1.030{nominal}
pH of Urine by Test strip6.65.5 - 7.0pH
Protein [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip5.80 - 20mg/dL
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine nitrite positive
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine blood test = +
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine leukocyte test
Leukocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field45.1 H0 - 5/[HPF]
Erythrocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field2.70 - 5/[HPF]
Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field1.90 - 10/[HPF]
Casts [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyUrine microscopy: no casts
Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyMucus in urine
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power fieldFinding of presence of bacteria

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The findings indicate a urinary tract infection (UTI) with significant pyuria, bacteriuria, positive nitrites, and hematuria, confirmed by >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli in urine culture. The presence of glucose in urine may suggest hyperglycemia or impaired renal threshold. Low urine specific gravity could indicate dilute urine. Recommend clinical correlation and appropriate antimicrobial therapy.
